speaker: narrator
text: The mountain smelled of hot stone and a kiln that had just shut its mouth. Caelin woke to Vex's palm over his mouth, her other hand clamped to his shoulder.

speaker: unknown
text: "Don't make a sound,"

speaker: narrator
text: she breathed.

speaker: unknown
text: "Company."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale in his right forearm tightened its pulse, not warning, not ease -- just braced. Coal-glow slid toward ember-red; the fine obsidian filaments beneath his skin crawled up toward the elbow like roots searching stone.

speaker: narrator
text: From the lower passage -- the one they'd marked with chalk and left closed -- voices braided the air. Wrong, guttural, harmonized like things that had practiced speaking together inside the same throat.

speaker: unknown
text: "Ember-born..."

speaker: unknown
text: "...the chain calls..."

speaker: unknown
text: "...submit or be consumed..."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's hand left his mouth.

speaker: unknown
text: "Up,"

speaker: narrator
text: she hissed, and the camp came alive in a soft clatter of straps and breath.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana rolled to her feet, sword in one hand, the other kindling silver-gold. The light stuttered gold?silver?gold -- a tremor she smoothed with breath. Thornik's pack went from hum to urgent whine as needles spun.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's vine-marks dimmed to a determined green. Nyxara stood in a movement that wasn't one, purple aura guttering like a candle in wind. Durgan took the choke point, blades bare, shadow pooled neat at his boots -- too neat.

speaker: vex
text: "Signatures?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex murmured.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik checked a gauge that vibrated his teeth.

speaker: vex
text: "Multiple. Same resonance as Thornwick -- stronger. Focused."

speaker: nyxara
text: "They'll smell the Ember,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, eyes on Caelin's arm.

speaker: vex
text: "A bonfire in a dark valley."

speaker: serana
text: "Can you dim it?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked, already moving to shield the center.

speaker: vex
text: "No."

speaker: narrator
text: He'd tried. White sparks punished lies, and it punished denial just as fast.

speaker: serana
text: "Then we work with it,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said.

speaker: vex
text: "Caelin center-back. I hold the line. Thornik on my flank, steel first, toys second. Vex wide right. Elowen disrupt their approach. Nyxara -- "

speaker: nyxara
text: "I'll... persuade,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara murmured, voice a soft knife.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan didn't wait for orders.

speaker: vex
text: "They know exactly where we are,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, jaw tight.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Kill fast, before the trap closes."

speaker: narrator
text: The voices fell silent. The air drew a breath.

speaker: narrator
text: By first light that never reached this deep, the tunnels had learned their names.

speaker: narrator
text: The hum settled to a floor-note you only noticed when it stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: It stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: Dust lifted off the stone in slow spirals, then hung as if waiting to be told where to fall.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's hand found the wall.

speaker: vex
text: "Metallic again,"

speaker: narrator
text: she murmured.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Coin on the tongue."

speaker: durgan
text: "The pull's stronger,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said. Three words. Nothing more.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's wardline thinned to chalk-pale, then steadied.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin felt the scale tighten -- warning without pain, the kind that comes just before it hurts.

speaker: narrator
text: The chamber ahead was the shape of a held breath.

speaker: narrator
text: Shadow clung to the facets and then decided to stand.

speaker: narrator
text: Five of them -- wrong-jointed, glass-slick, learning their limbs like newborns. They moved like puppets given too many strings. Joints bent a fraction wrong.

speaker: narrator
text: Skin was the color of cold parchment, stretched and shifting over bones that didn't respect anatomy. Eyes were black from lid to lid -- no iris, no white, just void that stared back.

speaker: narrator
text: Their shadows moved first, crawling toward Caelin's arm with hungry, fingered curiosity.

speaker: narrator
text: When they spoke, three voices braided through each throat: the people they had been, something ancient and patient, and beneath both the grind of stone on stone.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Ember-born,"

speaker: narrator
text: they said together.

speaker: durgan
text: "The Seal remembers. The Seal hungers."

speaker: serana
text: "Line,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, sword low.

speaker: durgan
text: "Light, then edges. No heroes."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik palmed two charges.

speaker: serana
text: "Edges I have. Heroes, sadly, I left at home."

speaker: vex
text: "Left,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex snapped, already moving.

speaker: serana
text: "Right,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara echoed, voice silk over wire.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin took center because the path in his bones put him there.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's edge bloomed black-violet -- ink spreading through obsidian water -- and snapped back to red-gold. It hurt different this time: not the compressive burn of claiming, not the constant ache he could now ignore.

speaker: narrator
text: This was a hook, barbed and patient, tugging along the filament lattice as if it could pull the Ember out of him with steady, surgical malice.

speaker: narrator
text: He staggered. Elowen caught his shoulder.

speaker: vex
text: "I can sense it,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, voice tight.

speaker: serana
text: "A severing. I can't ease this."

speaker: serana
text: "Then we break the hand holding the hook,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, and ran.

speaker: narrator
text: He drew heat and starved it to a hard white seam across his palm, then cut the closest shadow at the ankle.

speaker: narrator
text: It shrieked in three registers and folded -- but did not die.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain lit the scale a murderous red. He kept his hand closed anyway.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana hit them like a wall of weather. Shield-first, a ward-wave plowing the nearest thrall backward so hard its shadow recoiled, hissing. Her sword came down in a clean kill-line --

speaker: narrator
text: Her lips moved -- automatic prayer, the kind learned in bone.

speaker: narrator
text: The answer struck from two mouths at once.

speaker: narrator
text: Protect the flame.

speaker: narrator
text: Break the vessel to save the flame.

speaker: narrator
text: Her wardlight guttered, flared, guttered. Blood salted her tongue.

speaker: vex
text: "Choose,"

speaker: narrator
text: she hissed -- to the voices, to herself. The blade came up anyway.

speaker: serana
text: "Fine. I choose now."

speaker: narrator
text: She stepped in and set her sword through the thing's shoulder -- and met resistance like cutting water that didn't agree to be cut. Light seared black flesh; it didn't fall. It grinned too wide -- teeth blooming like fungus where a mouth should stop.

speaker: vex
text: "Hard to kill!"

speaker: narrator
text: she called.

speaker: vex
text: "News flash,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, already gone from where she'd spoken.

speaker: thornik
text: "Duck,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, and rolled a thimble charge.

speaker: narrator
text: It coughed instead of boomed; the echo slapped the chamber flat.

speaker: narrator
text: The shadow's edge tore like wet paper where the sound hit it.

speaker: vex
text: "Acoustic shear,"

speaker: narrator
text: he breathed.

speaker: thornik
text: "Repeatable."

speaker: narrator
text: She was a blur on the fringe: two quick stabs behind a knee and a tendon, twist and away. The thrall buckled correctly -- then its shadow lashed like a whip and caught her across the ribs.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex hit stone, slid, swore without wasting air.

speaker: vex
text: "Shadows bite! Noted!"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex went low, a fast insult of steel at what passed for a heel.

speaker: thornik
text: "Trip the legs, quiet the mouth."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara didn't draw. She spoke a word that bent the lantern-angle; light arrived from the wrong direction.

speaker: narrator
text: The shadow flinched from illumination that shouldn't exist there.

speaker: vex
text: "Transposition,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, almost kindly.

speaker: thornik
text: "Stand in your proper place."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik roared -- a sound that came from under mountains -- and hit the hamstrung thrall with two axes like punctuation. Steel met neck. Head rolled. Its shadow screamed like glass breaking underwater and blew away.

speaker: vex
text: "One!"

speaker: narrator
text: he bellowed, beard alight with reflected ember.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara didn't shout. She breathed, and indecision bloomed in two corrupted minds. One raked at the wrong air where Caelin had stood; the other hesitated in a three-second loop of doubt.

speaker: thornik
text: "Now,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, soft but absolute.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana obliged. Silver-gold through the throat; the shadow boiled like ice in summer and was gone.

speaker: narrator
text: The fourth rose for Vex's throat.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan was there. No shout, no flourish -- steel where it had to be. His forearm took the rake that would have opened her.

speaker: narrator
text: He shoved it back two steps and did not pursue.

speaker: narrator
text: The last turned from the fighters and stretched too many fingers toward Caelin.

speaker: vex
text: "Ember-born,"

speaker: narrator
text: three voices purred.

speaker: thornik
text: "The chain is mercy. Let us hold your fire. We will take the pain away."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale chose.

speaker: narrator
text: Heat flowered in his chest like a furnace door thrown wide. It wasn't temperature; it was pressure finding a path. His right arm lifted with a puppet's surety. Obsidian hex flared white. The lattice lit like molten wire from palm to collarbone.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't aim. Didn't choose. The Ember chose him as an answer.

speaker: narrator
text: Red-gold plasma ripped the air. Granite vitrified to glossy black where it touched. The smell hit hard -- hot glass, burnt ozone, iron filings on the tongue.

speaker: narrator
text: The thrall didn't burn. It unmade. Flesh evaporated. Bone sifted to ash. The shadow screamed in three notes and was not.

speaker: narrator
text: Three seconds. Then the door slammed shut.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain took the room.

speaker: narrator
text: The remaining shadows collapsed into behaving dark, dragging cold with them that wasn't temperature so much as absence.

speaker: narrator
text: They said nothing more. They only learned the shape of the group and thinned into the walls.

speaker: narrator
text: Sound returned in layers -- first to the floor, then to the air, then, last of all, to breath.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin flexed scorched fingers. The scale tallied pain and settled to coal-glow.

speaker: narrator
text: Sound wavered, distant and too loud. His heart rattled a panicked rhythm. The plate in his arm dropped from white-hot to furious red, the filaments pulsing like infected seams. His lungs forgot how. He went to a knee on a floor that looked like a mirror still cooling.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen was there, palms hovering. Green light reached for him -- and guttered, the smell of scorched leaves sharp and wrong. A thin edge of one vine-mark blackened on her skin as she pulled back.

speaker: vex
text: "I can't,"

speaker: narrator
text: she whispered, horrified.

speaker: thornik
text: "Post-cast residue burns my connection. I can ease your baseline, not this."

speaker: vex
text: "S'okay,"

speaker: narrator
text: he managed through his teeth.

speaker: thornik
text: "You told me."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex slid in hard, fingers at his pulse, pupils, breath.

speaker: vex
text: "Not dying,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said briskly.

speaker: thornik
text: "Shouldn't cast again for hours -- maybe a day -- unless we want him to."

speaker: narrator
text: She made a leveling motion with her hand.

speaker: vex
text: "You know."

speaker: narrator
text: Her joke was a little too thin to be a joke. He almost laughed anyway.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana kept her hand on the ward until it stopped shaking. When she finally sheathed the blade, her mouth was set against words.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex wiped her knife clean and didn't thank Durgan. She didn't not thank him, either.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara smoothed her coat as if dirt were an opinion, then watched Serana a fraction too long.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik counted fuses he hadn't used and grinned like a man who'd just learned his math could kill.

speaker: narrator
text: He glanced at Caelin's arm -- coal-glow again, the ember-motes drifting slow.

speaker: thornik
text: "Back to baseline,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, as if noting a reading.

speaker: vex
text: "Coal means steady. Ember-red means spent. Black-violet means -- "

speaker: narrator
text: He stopped himself.

speaker: thornik
text: "Well. We know what black-violet means."

speaker: narrator
text: At the mouth of the passage, Durgan hadn't moved an inch. He kept the retreat open, blades ready -- eyes trained not on the dark beyond, but on the lattice burning under Caelin's skin. His shadow flexed like a dog that had caught a scent.

speaker: narrator
text: He flattened it with will and left crescent bruises through his trousers where his fingers anchored him.

speaker: narrator
text: The hum returned, deeper, almost pleased.

speaker: caelin
text: "Not a voice,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, listening to the stone.

speaker: thornik
text: "Not yet."

speaker: narrator
text: They took stock fast because there was no slow left.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex: moving, rib-shot, functioning at perhaps 70%.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik: winded, uncut, checking his instruments with shaking hands.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen: whole in body, less in magic -- one vine-mark dim at the edge, blackened where she'd tried to touch the post-cast burn.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara: unmarked, a faint frost halo gone when she blinked.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan: coiled too tight, shadow obedient by force, four crescent marks pressed into his thigh through his trousers.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana: connection steadied, a tiny flicker when she turned to speak, lips still tasting copper from the split counsel.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin: vertical by stubbornness, the scale back to coal-glow, pain settling to the constant ache Elowen could ease later -- if it wasn't fresh-burn.

speaker: nyxara
text: "They'll follow the Ember,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, breaking the silence.

speaker: thornik
text: "Distance won't matter."

speaker: vex
text: "Surface,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex started -- then glanced toward the south, toward Millbrook and the scattered hamlets tucked under these ridges. Her jaw tightened.

speaker: thornik
text: "No. Not with a beacon on two legs. I hate the idea, but... containment."

speaker: thornik
text: "The Undervault,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik offered, already pulling the battered journal from his coat. Pages crackled, stained with old blood and older ink.

speaker: vex
text: "Pre-Concord passages. Old dwarf-wards. My grandfather mapped entries and marked the Deep Hall. Wards there might confuse whatever is sniffing us."

speaker: serana
text: "Safer for civilians,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, jaw set.

speaker: vex
text: "Not for us."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's gaze went inward, the way it did when she listened to the Weave. The green of her tattoos pulsed faintly, then dimmed.

speaker: serana
text: "The deep places are hurt. Wounded things can be generous. Or angry."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's fingers twitched toward a blade, then away -- a motion so fast only Vex caught it.

speaker: vex
text: "Safer to be hunted in stone than among farms."

speaker: narrator
text: They looked to Caelin.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale warmed -- not push, not pull. Waiting.

speaker: narrator
text: He thought of Thornwick. Of glass bones where houses had stood. Of Mira's small hand clutching his ruined cloak.

speaker: serana
text: "Undervault,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "We don't bring this down to doorsteps."

speaker: thornik
text: "Excellent,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, already shouldering weight, adjusting straps with practiced efficiency.

speaker: vex
text: "I've always wanted to risk my life on decayed ventilation and ancestral pride."

speaker: vex
text: "Lead on,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, checking her daggers with methodical precision.

speaker: thornik
text: "If a ward eats us, I'm haunting you."

speaker: thornik
text: "Queue forms to the left,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, finding his cheer again despite everything.

speaker: vex
text: "Ancestral disapproval is first-come, first-served."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik found the seam in the wall by feel -- three high, two low, one center -- and an ancient hinge sighed cold air into their faces. The breath of the Undervault tasted of iron, dust, and old smoke that had forgotten its fire.

speaker: narrator
text: A tight spiral took them down past shelves of fossilized lichen that glowed just enough to make shadows mean. The scale's coal-glow mixed with green-blue, turning everything a strange bruised purple.

speaker: narrator
text: Their boots found perfect dwarf-cut treads, each one precisely placed, worn smooth by centuries of use. Breath plumed in the cold.

speaker: narrator
text: They came to a three-way junction.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik thumbed brittle pages, following his grandfather's diagrams with one finger while holding his lantern close.

speaker: thornik
text: "Left is sealed. Collapse, bad air, and a page of do not in runes only grandmothers use."

speaker: narrator
text: He squinted at faded ink.

speaker: vex
text: "Right's toward the forges -- likely flooded. Straight is the Deep Hall. Strongest wards. Multiple exits."

speaker: vex
text: "'Should be' is the most dangerous phrase,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex muttered, testing the air currents with a wetted finger.

speaker: thornik
text: "Aye,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said cheerfully.

speaker: vex
text: "Shall we?"

speaker: narrator
text: They walked until silence felt heavier than noise.

speaker: narrator
text: The mountain had gone from predator to cathedral -- huge, patient, and indifferent.

speaker: narrator
text: Every sound came back doubled, as if the tunnels were practicing conversation.

speaker: narrator
text: By the time they moved again, their footsteps felt borrowed.

speaker: narrator
text: The bridge waited with the patience of stone made to sing.

speaker: narrator
text: Below, the dark had depth -- thick enough to hold a choir.

speaker: serana
text: "Single file,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, testing the first plank with her boot.

speaker: vex
text: "Slow. No idle noise."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik swallowed a theory about resonant architecture. Vex swallowed a curse about trusting ancient engineering.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said nothing and stepped where he was told.

speaker: narrator
text: The first footfall rang a low bell -- pure, round, unhurried.

speaker: narrator
text: The second answered a third below, like harmony remembering itself.

speaker: narrator
text: By Caelin's fourth step the bells had learned his weight and offered a scale that climbed in perfect intervals.

speaker: narrator
text: The plate in his arm kept time despite him, pulsing in rhythm with the bells -- coal-glow brightening fractionally with each step.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway across, Nyxara's heel grazed a loose shard.

speaker: narrator
text: The note that answered came bent, then split into two, then four -- ugly, eager, dissonant.

speaker: narrator
text: The span trembled. Cracks webbed outward from where her foot had landed, then held -- barely.

speaker: vex
text: "Don't improvise,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex hissed without turning, knuckles white on the rope.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Tragic,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, adjusting her footing with careful precision.

speaker: vex
text: "I excel at it."

speaker: narrator
text: From the chasm rose an answering chord -- soft at first, then full enough to live in. Harmonics layered upon harmonics, building a cathedral of sound in the empty air.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's eyes went wide.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Roots sing under storms,"

speaker: narrator
text: she whispered.

speaker: vex
text: "This isn't that."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's hand hovered over his tuning fork, then curled to a fist.

speaker: nyxara
text: "This is engineered. Intentional. The entire bridge is an instrument."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin tasted metal and old ash. The scale warmed without pain, as if listening back, recognizing something in the music.

speaker: narrator
text: They reached a split in the span: two narrow runs diverging for ten feet before rejoining, creating a gap where no single person could span both paths.

speaker: thornik
text: "Pairs, or it drops us,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, staring at the bell spacing carved into each path.

speaker: nyxara
text: "It wants balance, not mass. Weight distribution."

speaker: thornik
text: "Vex with Nyxara,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana ordered, already assessing.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Thornik with me. Caelin in the center. Durgan anchors."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex clicked her tongue.

speaker: thornik
text: "You do love a trust exercise."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara smiled.

speaker: nyxara
text: "I adore being trusted."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan took the rear without comment, his shadow pooling perfectly still despite the chasm wind.

speaker: narrator
text: The twin paths learned their pairs fast -- two notes for each step, tuned just off a perfect fifth. When Serana's breath hitched mid-crossing, her note wavered. The bridge shuddered.

speaker: caelin
text: "Hold,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said -- more plea than order.

speaker: narrator
text: She held. The bells forgave her, the dissonance resolving back to harmony.

speaker: narrator
text: A hairline seam opened under Vex's foot without warning.

speaker: narrator
text: She didn't scream; she vanished down to the knee, fingers scrabbling for purchase. Her knife hand caught stone, blade sinking into a crack with desperate precision.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara didn't reach for her. She changed the angle of her shadow with a subtle gesture, fingers tracing sigils in the air.

speaker: narrator
text: For one breath, light came from the wrong direction; the stone remembered being whole. The seam closed just enough.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex kicked free, hauled up with trembling arms, swore once -- the rare, sincere kind that meant actual fear.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them Durgan's note didn't change. He hadn't moved. Hadn't even shifted his weight.

speaker: narrator
text: The last bell rang itself when Caelin stepped off the stone, a final perfect tone that lingered in the air long after it should have faded.

speaker: narrator
text: Ahead, the corridor opened into a dome vast enough to hold weather.

speaker: narrator
text: Nine pillars ringed the space, each cut with a different pattern of facets that caught light and swallowed it. They didn't glow. They absorbed.

speaker: narrator
text: The quiet here was thicker than dark.

speaker: thornik
text: "Resonant sink,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik breathed, voice dropping to a whisper instinctively.

speaker: caelin
text: "It drinks sound."

speaker: vex
text: "Then whisper,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: caelin
text: "Or think louder than you speak."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's wardlight dimmed to a candle's stubborn core.

speaker: vex
text: "This place keeps oaths. Or breaks them."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara walked the nearest pillar, fingertip hovering, never touching.

speaker: caelin
text: "Nine doors that forgot they were doors."

speaker: durgan
text: "The pull's stronger,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said. Three words. Nothing more.

speaker: narrator
text: The plate brightened from coal to ember.

speaker: narrator
text: Not pain -- attendance.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin laid two fingers against the nearest pillar. It did not warm. It did not sing.

speaker: narrator
text: It remembered a heat so old the stone no longer called it heat.

speaker: narrator
text: In that heartbeat he saw a hand not his own pressing this same facet; a voice not his own saying a name he didn't know how to repeat. The vision smelled of forge-fire and ozone, tasted of copper and purpose.

speaker: narrator
text: He pulled back. Taste of copper. Taste of thunder about to happen.

speaker: caelin
text: "I think it knows the order,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: durgan
text: "I don't."

speaker: narrator
text: A note rose from somewhere beneath the dome -- lower than any bell on the span, the kind that rearranges ribs and makes bones remember they're temporary.

speaker: narrator
text: The nine pillars answered by not answering. Each drank the sound and gave back silence, creating a void where the note should have echoed.

speaker: thornik
text: "Echoes below,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, instruments beginning to hum in sympathy.

speaker: elowen
text: "Not echoes,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen murmured, one hand pressed to her sternum as if feeling the vibration there.

speaker: thornik
text: "Something that expects us."

speaker: narrator
text: They stood in the quiet a moment longer, counting breaths they hadn't meant to hold.

speaker: narrator
text: Then the pull in Caelin's arm tightened, and the path chose left.

speaker: narrator
text: The Deep Hall opened not gradually, but all at once -- as if a cathedral of stone had been hiding an arm's length beyond the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: Pillars like old oaks rose from an obsidian floor polished to mirror.

speaker: narrator
text: Bas-reliefs coiled up each: dwarves and dragons working side by side; forges burning in colors metal shouldn't make; the nine pedestals carved and blessed, each one distinct in its geometry.

speaker: narrator
text: At the crown of every pillar, chains looped around a shape no artist would define -- something that might have been a lock, or a heart, or both.

speaker: narrator
text: Along the walls, nine alcoves expressed their schools in stone:

speaker: narrator
text: - Evocation: facets that multiplied light until it looked like heat, angular and aggressive.

speaker: narrator
text: - Abjuration: nested bevels, each guarding the next in concentric protection.

speaker: narrator
text: - Conjuration: empty arches promising somewhere else, frames without destinations.

speaker: narrator
text: - Divination: spirals your eyes couldn't follow without falling, probability made geometry.

speaker: narrator
text: - Enchantment: curves that made attention lean, seductive and subtle.

speaker: narrator
text: - Illusion: surfaces that adjusted when you didn't look, never quite the same twice.

speaker: narrator
text: - Necromancy: a hollow; an absence; a worn edge where something used to be.

speaker: narrator
text: - Transmutation: rough stone flowing to glass mid-surface, frozen mid-change.

speaker: narrator
text: - Destruction: edges broken on purpose to prove a point, jagged and defiant.

speaker: narrator
text: In the center, a round granite table big enough for a parliament. Dust softened the shine. No footprints but theirs.

speaker: thornik
text: "The wards held,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik breathed, watching his scanner's needles settle to a calm green.

speaker: elowen
text: "Active peace. Saints and stone -- this place is safe."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana still paced the perimeter -- old habits dying hard. Her light swept high vaults and alcoves; nothing moved but breath and dust motes dancing in the ward-glow. Vex tested for pressure plates out of reflex, found none. Elowen's voice came out as reverent as prayer.

speaker: thornik
text: "Stone remembers bargains."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan paused at the threshold. The carved runes flickered -- curious, then tolerant.

speaker: elowen
text: "Uncomfortable,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said honestly, and crossed. His shadow tried to widen past his boots; the ward-light nudged it back like a shepherd dog redirecting wayward sheep. He set his jaw. It obeyed.

speaker: thornik
text: "We rest,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana decided.

speaker: elowen
text: "Pairs on watch. No solos."

speaker: narrator
text: They burned a small, clean fire in a shallow stone cradle with invisible vents -- Thornik tapped the pillar and grinned:

speaker: thornik
text: "We make smoke go where we want. Grandfather's diagrams called this 'breathstone.' Channels run through the walls."

speaker: narrator
text: The air tasted faintly of oil and old ash; fresher scents -- leather, metal polish, juniper from a scrap of kindling -- cut through the mineral cold.

speaker: narrator
text: They ate dried meat and hard cheese. They checked steel, adjusted straps, cleaned blades. They didn't talk much at first.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin sat with his back to a pillar beneath Evocation, watching the facets multiply the firelight into something that looked almost like the flames he carried.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale had cooled to its steady coal-glow; the constant ache was back -- the kind Elowen could ease when it wasn't fresh-burn. He flexed his hand. The lattice answered, not offended, not appeased.

speaker: narrator
text: You'll know when you can pay again, it seemed to say.

speaker: narrator
text: He hoped his body would warn him before his bone-marrow did.

speaker: narrator
text: First Watch: Caelin & Vex

speaker: narrator
text: They kept it simple: steel on knees, backs to stone, ears tuned to the empty halls. The scale pulsed with his heart, ember drifting like slow fish under dark glass.

speaker: vex
text: "That thing is you now,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, not unkind, studying the plate where it lay flush in his skin.

speaker: thornik
text: "Optional to like it. Not optional to learn it."

speaker: narrator
text: He huffed a laugh.

speaker: vex
text: "It calls me out when I lie. When anyone does."

speaker: narrator
text: He nodded at his arm.

speaker: thornik
text: "White spark. Like thorns under the skin."

speaker: vex
text: "Handy and infuriating."

speaker: narrator
text: She studied him with those mismatched eyes.

speaker: thornik
text: "For what it's worth -- you didn't hesitate when it acted. Courage or stupidity."

speaker: vex
text: "Which do you vote?"

speaker: thornik
text: "Yes."

speaker: narrator
text: She eased a breath around her ribs, wincing slightly.

speaker: vex
text: "Brave people do stupid things with better reasons."

speaker: thornik
text: "And you joke so you don't scream."

speaker: vex
text: "Don't get romantic. Joking also annoys monsters."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale stayed quiet. Truth accepted.

speaker: narrator
text: They sat in companionable silence after that, listening to the fire pop and the mountain breathe.

speaker: narrator
text: Second Watch: Serana & Thornik

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's hands rested in prayer form, fingers laced. No words came. Her lips moved occasionally but made no sound. Thornik pretended to tune his scanner while watching her not-pray.

speaker: thornik
text: "Still split?"

speaker: narrator
text: he asked gently after a long silence.

speaker: vex
text: "Protect yesterday. Sacrifice today."

speaker: narrator
text: Her mouth crooked, humorless.

speaker: thornik
text: "I can follow orders. I cannot follow contradictions."

speaker: vex
text: "In a good system, contradictions signal a changed input."

speaker: narrator
text: He tapped his grandfather's journal.

speaker: thornik
text: "He wrote: Balance isn't the absence of cost. It's paying without bankrupting the world."

speaker: vex
text: "Doesn't make it easier."

speaker: thornik
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said softly.

speaker: vex
text: "But it makes it necessary."

speaker: narrator
text: Her light flickered gold?silver?gold again; she steadied it with a breath and nodded, accepting the truth if not the comfort of it.

speaker: narrator
text: They sat watching the shadows dance on ancient carvings, and neither spoke of the weight they carried.

speaker: narrator
text: Third Watch: Nyxara & Elowen

speaker: narrator
text: Silence sat between them like a cat that owned the room.

speaker: elowen
text: "Your magic is... angled,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen said at last, breaking the quiet.

speaker: nyxara
text: "So is yours,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara replied, head tilted with feline curiosity.

speaker: elowen
text: "Yours bends toward growth and cycles. Mine bends toward... consent, of a kind."

speaker: nyxara
text: "Do you trust it?"

speaker: elowen
text: "Trust implies parity."

speaker: narrator
text: A small, tired smile.

speaker: nyxara
text: "I honor the bargains I make."

speaker: elowen
text: "I'm losing ground,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen said softly, touching the edge of the blackened vine-mark with careful fingers.

speaker: nyxara
text: "When he casts, I can't help. I burn."

speaker: nyxara
text: "Then plan for loss,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said. Not cruel. Precise.

speaker: elowen
text: "Decide who you are when the Weave doesn't answer."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen looked at the Necromancy alcove without fear, studying the hollow where something had been carved away.

speaker: nyxara
text: "I don't know."

speaker: elowen
text: "Decide anyway."

speaker: narrator
text: A frost halo settled on Nyxara's lashes for a heartbeat and melted, leaving tiny droplets like tears that weren't.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen noticed. Said nothing.

speaker: narrator
text: Fourth Watch: Durgan (breaking the pair rule)

speaker: narrator
text: He took the arch alone and didn't wake the person assigned to him. Not defiance -- necessity. When he slept, his shadow got ideas.

speaker: narrator
text: He kept it small, neat, obedient by force, the way you keep your breathing even under a predator's gaze. The scale's distant glow tugged at his periphery like a road that always slopes downward, whispering promises in a language he refused to learn.

speaker: narrator
text: Walk away, a voice inside offered. Lose yourself in the dark. Stop being a danger.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't. Instead he squeezed four half-moons into his thigh through wool and felt the pain anchor him. Added four more next to the earlier set, a constellation of control etched in bruises.

speaker: narrator
text: His shadow moved anyway.

speaker: narrator
text: Not far -- an inch, maybe two, stretching toward the coal-glow at the edge of Caelin's bedroll. He caught it the moment he felt the resistance ease, snapped it back with a breath so sharp it hurt his chest, and held it there. It obeyed. Barely.

speaker: narrator
text: He made reaching boring. Made it cost. But it had moved on its own, and they both knew it.

speaker: nyxara
text: "How long,"

speaker: narrator
text: he thought, not asking anyone,

speaker: elowen
text: "before I can't hold it still?"

speaker: narrator
text: The ward-lines along the threshold pulsed once -- assessing, not judging. They recognized restraint when they saw it.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't move.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin woke to a sound his bones recognized before his mind did.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud.

speaker: narrator
text: Not weather. Not rockfall. The counting of something chained far below.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale answered with a brighter coal-glow; ember drift quickened inside the obsidian. Around the circle, heads lifted one by one. The sound came again, nearer -- or just more focused, as if attention had turned their direction.

speaker: narrator
text: Thud-thud-thud-thud --

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's needles jittered from calm green to anxious yellow.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Movement. Multiple. Below us. Above us. Possibly... through us?"

speaker: vex
text: "Any other good news?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked, already on her feet, daggers appearing in her hands.

speaker: thornik
text: "The ventilation is superb,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, shouldering his pack with practiced speed.

speaker: vex
text: "And I'm very attached to my head."

speaker: narrator
text: From the three corridors -- the sealed, the flooded, the safe -- stone began to move. Grinding. Opening. The air drew a breath that wasn't theirs.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana rose fully armored, sword in hand, voice calm to keep the pulse down in others.

speaker: thornik
text: "We move now. Thornik on point. Same formation. If a ward asks for courtesy, we give it. If something tries to herd us -- "

speaker: vex
text: "We bite the hand,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, rolling a shoulder and wincing as her ribs protested.

speaker: thornik
text: "Then run."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stood. The ache was a live thing in his bones. Elowen's palm hovered near his arm -- she tested the air for fresh-burn and found none. Relief drifted in like cool rain; the pain stepped back a pace, manageable again.

speaker: narrator
text: He swallowed gratitude, because speaking it would make it real, and he didn't know how many more times he'd get it.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan slid to rear-guard. His shadow pooled obedi --

speaker: narrator
text: No. It pressed at the edges like tide against a seawall. He smoothed it with breath and didn't look at Caelin, didn't acknowledge the way it wanted to reach toward the coal-glow in the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara passed the Evocation alcove and paused for a heartbeat, head tilted. The scale gave a silver shimmer -- a brief halo that said another magic is looking back. She smiled to herself and moved on.

speaker: narrator
text: They stepped toward the straight corridor. Behind them, in the polished seal of the Deep Hall's floor, their reflections delayed by a single blink -- there, then gone, as if the stone was deciding whether to remember them.

speaker: narrator
text: The heartbeat below picked up, counting them like beads on a string.

speaker: narrator
text: They went up.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved quickly after that, not running -- no one wanted to find out what would give chase -- but with the shared precision of people who'd heard a door close behind them and didn't know whose house they were in.

speaker: narrator
text: The tunnels felt steeper on the way out, as if the mountain had grown new muscles while they weren't watching. Gravity pulled harder, or perhaps exhaustion made it feel that way.

speaker: narrator
text: Walls that had been smooth now carried shallow grooves -- fresh, unfinished work, stone still warm under fingertips that brushed too close.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik muttered calculations under his breath, consulting his instruments.

speaker: vex
text: "Stress fractures don't self-heal. Something's rewriting geology."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex:

speaker: thornik
text: "Tell it to stop."

speaker: vex
text: "Would if I knew the syntax."

speaker: thornik
text: "Try screaming louder."

speaker: narrator
text: She smiled. No one else did.

speaker: narrator
text: At the first junction they hit a dead end that hadn't existed before. The floor sloped upward in a spiral of newly-cooled glass, the surface too perfect to be natural, too warm to be old.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana touched the seam where new met old.

speaker: vex
text: "It's sealing itself behind us."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara looked at the closing curve, studying it with professional interest.

speaker: thornik
text: "Or ahead of us. Perspective is the first casualty of divine architecture."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin set his palm against the wall; the scale pulsed once -- recognition, not alarm.

speaker: vex
text: "It's guiding, not trapping."

speaker: vex
text: "Comforting,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, checking over her shoulder at the way they'd come.

speaker: thornik
text: "Until it stops."

speaker: narrator
text: The second tunnel forked into shadow. A draft hissed through like breath through teeth, carrying the smell of deep earth and older dark.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana drew light. It came thin and trembling, barely enough to push back the shadows.

speaker: narrator
text: She tried again. The radiance stuttered, guttered, and went out like a candle in wind.

speaker: vex
text: "I... can't feel the source."

speaker: narrator
text: Her voice was calm because she'd trained it

speaker: narrator
text: to be, but her hand shook slightly before she lowered it.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's reply was all sugar.

speaker: thornik
text: "You're running on faith, darling. Check your reserves."

speaker: vex
text: "Enough."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's tone cut sharper than intended. The warmth of the scale spilled into his voice, unasked.

speaker: thornik
text: "She's tired, not empty."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana looked at him -- really looked -- and he saw in her eyes the flicker of gratitude and shame tangled together like roots and chains.

speaker: vex
text: "I'll manage."

speaker: narrator
text: She didn't.

speaker: narrator
text: They climbed a narrow chute where the roof had already dropped its first warning stones -- small ones, pebbles that clattered down like dice announcing the mountain's intentions.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik went first, hammering pitons into half-molten rock with steady, practiced strikes. Each blow echoed differently, telling him what the stone would hold and what it wouldn't.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway up, the air shifted -- pressure collapse, no sound, just a sudden absence -- then the wall shuddered.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin felt the pull in his arm flare white-hot.

speaker: thornik
text: "Move!"

speaker: narrator
text: They ran on instinct. The rear half of the tunnel folded inward, stone sighing like something exhaling dust and disappointment. Rock met rock with the sound of finality, sealing the way they'd come.

speaker: narrator
text: When the noise settled, the way back was gone.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik stared at the new wall, panting.

speaker: vex
text: "Correction: rewriting geology in real time."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex pressed her hand against the fresh barrier. Still warm.

speaker: thornik
text: "So we go forward or we stay here until we're decoration."

speaker: serana
text: "Forward,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, voice steady though her light had guttered out entirely.

speaker: thornik
text: "Always forward."

speaker: narrator
text: The climb stole what little rhythm remained in their breathing.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin's right arm ached with the memory of the sigils -- hot where the rest of him was cold. The filaments crawled beneath his skin like they were searching for something they'd lost.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen fell into step beside him without being asked.

speaker: serana
text: "You're shaking."

speaker: thornik
text: "It's the pull."

speaker: serana
text: "Then push back."

speaker: narrator
text: He managed a half-laugh.

speaker: thornik
text: "That easy?"

speaker: serana
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: thornik
text: "But start somewhere."

speaker: narrator
text: She brushed her hand near his sleeve; the ache dulled. Not gone -- just bearable. The green of her tattoos flickered, dimmed slightly, another small price paid.

speaker: serana
text: "Thank you."

speaker: thornik
text: "Later,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: serana
text: "When we're out."

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them Nyxara glanced at Durgan's shadow -- still too obedient, too smooth, like a dog trained to sit perfectly still while its master walked away.

speaker: thornik
text: "You hear it too?"

speaker: narrator
text: she asked quietly.

speaker: serana
text: "The hum?"

speaker: narrator
text: He shook his head once.

speaker: thornik
text: "Footsteps."

speaker: serana
text: "Whose?"

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't answer. Didn't look at her. Just kept walking with that deliberate, measured pace that suggested if he stopped, he might not start again.

speaker: narrator
text: When she looked again, his shadow was holding perfectly still while his boots moved -- a fraction too much control, the kind that comes from fighting something every single second.

speaker: narrator
text: At last the tunnel widened enough for rest.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik struck a flame that barely caught, nursing it like a dying thing. The light painted their faces in ochre and exhaustion, hollowing cheeks and deepening shadows.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex divided rations without asking; she had counted before anyone noticed they were running low. Dried meat, harder cheese, a swallow of water each. She moved with the efficiency of someone who'd learned hunger as a child and never forgotten the lesson.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana knelt apart, tracing the sigil of her order in ash on the ground. The mark wouldn't hold shape. The dust slid away from her fingers as if the mountain itself rejected the prayer. She tried again. Again the ash refused her.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin watched but didn't interrupt.

speaker: narrator
text: Some prayers, he thought, were best left unanswered.

speaker: narrator
text: A faint tremor moved through the stone like a muscle tensing.

speaker: narrator
text: Their gear jingled once, softly.

speaker: narrator
text: Then the hum changed key -- dropping a full tone, deeper, more present.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's head snapped up.

speaker: thornik
text: "That's not us."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen closed her eyes, listening with her whole body the way trees listen to approaching storms.

speaker: serana
text: "Something old is turning over in its sleep."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex sheathed her knife slow, deliberate.

speaker: thornik
text: "Let's not be the alarm clock."

speaker: narrator
text: They climbed the last stretch in silence.

speaker: narrator
text: Snowlight filtered down from a crack above -- thin, colorless, precious as air. The temperature dropped with every step, breath turning to visible ghosts in the suddenly frigid air.

speaker: narrator
text: When they emerged onto open rock, the air hit like resurrection: cold, clean, cruelly bright after so long in the dark.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin turned back once. The tunnel mouth had already begun to glass over, edges smoothing and sealing, faintly glowing from within with a light that pulsed in time with the distant counting below.

speaker: narrator
text: He couldn't tell if it was sealing itself shut or lighting a path back.

speaker: narrator
text: Either way, it watched them go.

speaker: elowen
text: "The mountain remembers,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen whispered.

speaker: thornik
text: "Question is -- what?"

speaker: narrator
text: Wind met them like a blade honed on snow.

speaker: narrator
text: The world was all angles -- black rock, white glare, a sky so close it felt like a lid pressing down on the peaks.

speaker: narrator
text: Breath came thin and loud in their own ears, each inhalation a small victory against the altitude.

speaker: narrator
text: The ridge ran like a spine, dropping to glazed couloirs on either side -- sheets of ice that fell away into clouds and nothing. One wrong step and gravity would have opinions.

speaker: narrator
text: Far ahead, the flame-path in Caelin's vision brightened to a fine wire, then split along the crest -- two threads, left and lower, right and steep.

speaker: narrator
text: He blinked; the right-hand thread tugged harder. Not safer. Just certain. The kind of certainty that didn't care about safety.

speaker: elowen
text: "Two ways,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "Then there's only one,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex answered, already squinting into the glare, reading the terrain with the practiced eye of someone who'd survived by always choosing the less-bad option.

speaker: narrator
text: Sunlight cut through high cloud and made three mock-suns at the horizon -- halos stacked like coins, a parhelion that shouldn't have been so clear at this time of day.

speaker: narrator
text: For a moment the middle ring threw nine spokes across the glare, a trick of ice crystals and angle.

speaker: narrator
text: They watched until the spokes thinned and died, melting back into ordinary light.

speaker: thornik
text: "Pretty,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, too softly.

speaker: vex
text: "I hate it."

speaker: vex
text: "Atmospheric phenomenon,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: thornik
text: "Ice crystals refracting -- "

speaker: thornik
text: "I know what it is,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik interrupted.

speaker: vex
text: "Doesn't make it less ominous."

speaker: serana
text: "Light for the edge,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, lifting a hand as they prepared to traverse the exposed ridge.

speaker: narrator
text: Nothing came.

speaker: narrator
text: She breathed, steadied, tried again. The wardline stuttered and guttered like breath caught in a throat, flickering silver-gold-silver before dying completely.

speaker: vex
text: "Source is... thin,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, voice even by force of will.

speaker: serana
text: "Can't hold it."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's smile didn't travel to her eyes.

speaker: vex
text: "Altitude does that to certainty."

speaker: caelin
text: "Enough,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said -- quieter than anger, firmer than plea.

speaker: vex
text: "We move slow. No miracles, just rope."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana nodded once and lowered her hand. That, too, was a kind of prayer -- accepting limitation, working within it.

speaker: narrator
text: They roped in -- Thornik's knots neat as script, each one tested twice. Vex testing every anchor with insults and physics, trusting neither completely.

speaker: narrator
text: The ridge narrowed to two boot-widths and a faith wider than comfort.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan took rear without comment. He walked like a man who had been here in other lifetimes and found the drop repetitive. His shadow stayed small, obedient, costly.

speaker: narrator
text: Halfway across, a gust hit sideways -- a wall of wind with malice in it. Elowen's heel skated on rime; Caelin felt the rope jar tight across his chest, fibers groaning.

speaker: caelin
text: "Lean in,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, not sure who needed it. Maybe himself.

speaker: narrator
text: They did. The gust passed. The ridge became a ridge again.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara altered the angle of her shadow with a single, almost lazy wrist-turn -- subtle magic, barely noticeable.

speaker: narrator
text: For one breath the glare softened where their eyes needed it most, giving them a moment of relief from the sun-blindness.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex saw it and didn't comment. She only moved faster, wanting to be off the exposed spine before the mountain changed its mind.

speaker: durgan
text: "Shelter,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said, three heartbeats later.

speaker: narrator
text: Not warning. A direction.

speaker: narrator
text: They followed his chin-tilt to a bite taken out of the lee side -- a snow-scooped alcove backed by stone, protected from the worst of the wind.

speaker: narrator
text: They crouched out of the wind, bodies grateful for even this small mercy. Fire was a joke up here where oxygen barely existed; Thornik didn't try. They huddled together for warmth instead.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana sat with her back to rock and closed her eyes like someone setting down a weight too heavy to carry any longer.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin heard the whisper before he saw her jaw tighten -- the overlapping threads of two instructions that would never agree, playing in an endless loop.

speaker: narrator
text: Protect the flame.

speaker: narrator
text: Offer the bearer.

speaker: narrator
text: Her fingers found the chalk tucked in her belt and snapped it cleanly in two. The pieces fell into the snow and were immediately buried by a fresh gust.

speaker: caelin
text: "I can walk,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, eyes still shut.

speaker: durgan
text: "I can't promise more."

speaker: caelin
text: "That's enough,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin answered.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn't add, For now.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex distributed a stingy fistful of dried fruit as if paying out wages.

speaker: durgan
text: "Don't die; I'll have to redo headcount."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik chewed slowly, trying not to look at the halos still staining the horizon like cosmic warning signs.

speaker: caelin
text: "Quantitatively speaking, we're under-supplied on miracles."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara watched the way the wind curled around Caelin's forearm, as if the air had opinions about ownership.

speaker: durgan
text: "Quantitatively speaking,"

speaker: narrator
text: she murmured,

speaker: caelin
text: "we're over-supplied on audience."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan didn't sit. His shadow behaved itself. That felt like a decision that cost him.

speaker: narrator
text: The ridge underfoot hummed -- two notes a tone apart, beating slow against each other in a rhythm that made teeth ache.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik touched stone with a bare palm.

speaker: durgan
text: "That's not weather."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen listened with her whole body, the way she'd learned to read the forest's moods.

speaker: caelin
text: "Something turned over below us."

speaker: caelin
text: "Left,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said. The pull in his arm was no longer a suggestion. It was a command.

speaker: narrator
text: No one argued.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved into a light that felt older than noon, shadows stretching wrong directions, the sun pale and distant.

speaker: narrator
text: The mock-suns had faded, but the taste of metal hadn't -- copper and ozone, the flavor of lightning about to strike.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, the wind erased their prints in a single pass, as if the mountain wanted no record they'd been there.

speaker: narrator
text: Ahead, the path narrowed and began to sing -- a low, thrumming note that rose from stone or sky or the space between.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana opened her hand for light one more time and found none. She closed it before anyone saw, fingers curling into a fist.

speaker: narrator
text: But Caelin saw. And said nothing.

speaker: narrator
text: Before they moved, Vex ran her count aloud -- four charges spent, one blade chipped, three wounds that needed proper dressing when there was time. Thornik tapped the scale of remaining fuses against his thigh: six left.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen said nothing, but turned one wrist up; two more vine-marks had darkened at the edges, thin black lines where the green should have been. Serana's wardlight was gone and hadn't come back.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin looked at his arm. Coal-glow. Steady. Not spent, not surging -- just present.

speaker: durgan
text: "Still here,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, to no one in particular.

speaker: narrator
text: They descended.
